question_text: Explain why energy is described as 'wasted' as heat in machines, and how this relates to the conservation of energy.
correct_answer: Energy is not truly wasted; it is converted to heat through friction. This follows conservation of energy because the total energy input equals the useful energy output plus the heat energy produced.
explanation: Machines are not 100% efficient because friction converts some useful energy into thermal energy. The law of conservation of energy still applies—no energy is lost, only transformed into less useful forms.
